entries.title: Classified model as L1 Fusion
entries.description: The model performs Linear (no bias) + BiasAdd + ReLU in a straight-line computation with no data flow breaks. This is a classic fusion pattern (3 adjacent ops that can be fused). Classified at Level 1 (Fusion), not Level 2 (Layer), since there are no residual connections or repeated blocks.

entries.title: BiasAdd extracted as separate kernel from Linear
entries.description: The original model uses nn.Linear(bias=False) plus a separate nn.Parameter for the bias. This is intentionally separated (not using nn.Linear's built-in bias). The bias add is extracted as its own L0 kernel (bias_add_8192_fp32.py) with a learnable nn.Parameter, rather than merging it into the Linear kernel.

entries.title: ReLU classified as shape-independent elementwise kernel
entries.description: ReLU is an elementwise activation with no learnable parameters and no shape-dependent behavior. Named relu_fp32.py without shape suffix per the kernel naming convention for shape-independent ops.

entries.title: Linear kernel named with _nobias suffix
entries.description: The Linear operation uses bias=False, which is distinct from a standard Linear with bias. Named linear_8192x8192_nobias_fp32.py to clearly indicate this is a bias-free linear projection, differentiating it from a standard linear_8192x8192_fp32.py workload.

entries.title: Single decomposition step (L1 -> L0)
entries.description: Since the model is already at L1 (Fusion), only one decomposition step is needed: directly to L0 kernels. No intermediate L2 or L3 levels exist for this simple model.

entries.title: Verification passed with zero numerical difference
entries.description: verify_step.py confirmed exact numerical equivalence (max_diff=0.00e+00 across all 3 trials). Anti-cheat checks passed. The refactored model uses only child module calls in forward().
